titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the General Counsel, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday June 23, 2023 at 08:28

did.abstract: The records consist of correspondence, litigation files,research files, subject files, reports, court transcripts, and printed matterdetailing the work of the general counsel's office at Yale University.

corpname.part: Yale University. Office of the General Counsel

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 2-C (YRG2-C), Records documenting Yale legal affairs.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is restricted to the office of origin only.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced asofficial business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyrightstatus for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproductionof materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond thatallowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners.Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited withoutpermission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use restsexclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: General Counsel, Yale University, Records (RU 128). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged anddescribed at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does nofurther arrangement and description at the accession level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Therecords consist of correspondence, litigation files, research files, subject files, reports, court transcripts, and printed matter detailing the work of the general counsel's office at Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

corpname.part: Yale University. Office of the General Counsel

did.unittitle: Records for Greenburg v.Yale-New Haven Hospital

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: University Tribunal, Yale University, Records (RU 1115) contains the records of Sidney Altman as Chair of the University Tribunal from 1997-2001.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted to Office of Origin only
